task.json description. Verified via web search: This OID is the standard FHIR identifier system for French NIR (National Identification Register) per French healthcare FHIR standards (esante.gouv.fr). See https://smt.esante.gouv.fr/fhir/ and related documentation.

pi_items.verified_value: INCOMPLETE - task.json file is truncated and malformed
pi_items.source: File inspection: task.json has 1219 bytes, ends mid-string in description field. Contains 1 opening brace '{' but 0 closing braces '}'. Missing metadata field entirely. JSON parsing fails with 'Unterminated string' error.

pi_items.metadata_value: array of 8 patient objects with fields: family, given, gender, birthDate, city, postalCode, nir
pi_items.source: verifier.py line 32 tries to load: metadata.get('expected_patients', []) but metadata field does not exist in task.json. This will cause verifier to skip patient verification checks (lines 120-155 will iterate over empty array).

privileged_info_summary: The fhir_patient_export task extracts 8 synthetic French patient records from MedinTux DrTuxTest MySQL database and transforms them into FHIR R4 JSON Bundle format. Data is fully synthetic, created at runtime via SQL INSERT statements in setup_task.sh into tables IndexNomPrenom and fchpat. All 8 patients are well-formed with realistic French names, birth dates ranging from 1945-1990, correctly gender-coded (H=male, F=female), and NIR (French social security numbers) properly formatted as 15-digit codes with embedded birth year, month, and department information matching their cities. FHIR field mappings are correct: names mapped from database columns, gender values correctly translated to FHIR codes, dates in ISO 8601 format, addresses include French postal codes starting with department numbers (69=Lyon, 13=Marseille, 75=Paris, 31=Toulouse, 33=Bordeaux, 44=Nantes, 67=Strasbourg, 06=Nice), and identifier OID urn:oid:1.2.250.1.213.1.4.8 is correct for French NIR per esante.gouv.fr FHIR standards. CRITICAL DEFECT: task.json file is incomplete/malformed - truncated in the description field with missing metadata field that verifier.py expects. This prevents verifier from checking patient data accuracy (verifier will skip integrity checks with empty expected_patients array).
